sources: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP) Selection Criteria
NIH Research Training and Career Development Guidelines
Nature Careers: What Labs Look For in PhD Applicants

smart.label: Research Signal and Academic Rigor
what_counts: Relevant science major (Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Environmental Science, Marine Science, etc.)
Rigorous coursework: methods, statistics, lab techniques, upper-division science
GPA in major (3.0+ for industry; 3.5+ for competitive grad programs)
Honors thesis or independent research project

grit.label: Sustained Effort and Ownership
what_counts: Lab experience with clear duration (months/years) and role
Quantified impact: sample sizes, grant amounts, hours per week
Ownership of a project or sub-project within a lab
Teaching or mentoring in science (TA, tutor, outreach)

build.label: Evidence of Scientific Work
what_counts: Lab work with PI name, institution, and date range
Publications (even in prep or under review)
Research methods and techniques named explicitly
IRB or IACUC involvement where relevant
Field work, data collection, or analysis projects with outcomes

common_gaps.gap: No lab or research experience before senior year
common_gaps.fix: Reach out to professors for research assistantships. Many labs take undergraduates. Start with 5-10 hours/week and build. Even one semester of lab work is a baseline.

common_gaps.gap: Research bullets lack methods, scope, or outcomes
common_gaps.fix: Every research bullet needs: what you did (techniques, methods), scope (sample size, duration), and outcome (finding, publication, poster). Name the PI and lab.

common_gaps.gap: No quantified impact in research or lab roles
common_gaps.fix: Add numbers: hours per week, months in lab, sample size, grant amount, number of experiments, or data points analyzed.

common_gaps.gap: GPA below 3.0 with no compensating research signal
common_gaps.fix: Strong research (publication, sustained lab work) can offset GPA for grad programs. For industry, highlight technical skills and project outcomes.

common_gaps.gap: Only coursework, no independent or lab-based work
common_gaps.fix: Lab experience is the primary Build signal. Capstone or class projects help if they include methods and outcomes. Aim for at least one real lab role.
